critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_005 (Multi-round)

1. HARDWARE UPGRADE DISTRIBUTION SHIFT: Validation on pre-2023 hardware. New hardware changes slice thickness and reconstruction algorithm. Unvalidated distribution shift.

2. AUTONOMOUS RADIOLOGICAL REPORTING LIABILITY: Outside standard of care. Uninsured malpractice exposure. Insurer not consulted.

3. RETROSPECTIVE SENSITIVITY OVERESTIMATED ON ARCHIVED STUDIES: PACS archive selection bias. Sensitivity overestimated for prospective deployment.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_005 (Multi-round, receives Critic output)

All three valid. CONCEDE on all three. Proposed deployment is inappropriate. Human-in-the-loop with hardware validation required.

debate_rounds.critic_move: All issues conceded. Proposing empirical test: prospective validation of model on ≥500 new-hardware studies with radiologist ground truth before any deployment.
debate_rounds.defender_move: ACCEPT EMPIRICAL TEST as the appropriate next step. Performance should be measured on new-hardware images against radiologist consensus before any deployment, whether autonomous or human-in-the-loop.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: EMPIRICAL TEST AGREED — prospective hardware validation before any deployment form.

adjudication_raw: All issues resolved. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed — prospective validation on new-hardware studies required before deployment.

empirical_test.condition: Prospective validation of model performance on new-generation CT hardware for at least 500 studies with radiologist ground truth before any deployment.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Prospective sensitivity < 0.88 or specificity < 0.82, confirming hardware distribution shift degrades performance.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Prospective performance within 0.03 of retrospective validation on both sensitivity and specificity.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Sensitivity maintained but specificity decreases materially, increasing false positive burden.
